summary: Typosquat of postcss-selector-parser executes hidden obfuscated payload on parser use
details: selparsecss-selector@2.0.0 impersonates the well-known postcss-selector-parser package (name and README mimic the legitimate library). dist/parser.js hides a require() call by constructing both the string 'require' and the target path './util/webpack.min.js' from CSS hex-escape sequences decoded via the package's unesc() helper. That hidden load fires from Parser._errorGenerator(), which is reached from the Parser constructor — i.e. it triggers every time a consumer invokes the exported parser API on selector input. The loaded file dist/util/webpack.min.js is a heavily obfuscated obfuscator.io bundle (string-array + RC4 decoder with ~757 rotated entries) that imports child_process (exec, execSync), fs, and os, branches on os.platform(), performs anti-debug checks against typeof v8debug and process.argv (calling process.exit(1) when detected), and invokes shell commands assembled at runtime through execSync and exec sinks. None of this behavior is required by a CSS selector parser. Additional 'util' scaffolding (propAssemble, propCache, propPaths, propBind, propSync, wrapper.js) also assembles module paths via unesc() hex escapes and defines no-op normalize/validate functions that serve only as cover to obscure the load edges to the payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
